page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.se1.2xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated physical cores on an Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at 2.5 GHz with an x86_64 architecture. The instance is configured with 64.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates that the processor delivers modest performance. Single-core and multi-core CPU tests, such as stress-ng and Geekbench, place the instance in the bottom 25% of tested hardware. Cryptographic and compression tasks also show lower-tier performance, with single-threaded gzip compression scoring in the bottom 10%. However, the memory subsystem achieves average performance in large-block read and write operations, and the instance maintains average throughput in Redis SET operations and small-model LLM prompt processing.

The primary tradeoff of the ecs.se1.2xlarge is its high memory capacity paired with relatively weak compute performance. This resource density makes it a qualitative option for memory-bound workloads that do not require intensive CPU processing. It is suited for caching layers, lightweight database operations, and memory-intensive data processing tasks that can tolerate lower CPU clock speeds and baseline network throughput.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.se1.2xlarge is an x86_64 memory-optimized instance featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon 8163 physical cores running at 2.5 GHz, paired with 64.0 GB of RAM. It lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on a 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks reveal a clear tradeoff: while the instance exhibits weak single-core and multi-core CPU performance (ranking in the bottom 25% for stress-ng and Geekbench), its memory subsystem achieves average throughput during large-block read and write operations. Additionally, it maintains average performance in Redis SET operations and small-model LLM prompt processing. Given the high 8.0 GB of RAM per core ratio, this instance is configured for memory-intensive workloads with moderate compute requirements, such as caching layers, in-memory databases, and lightweight data processing tasks.
